issues.unsupported_claim: the real cycle involves more than these two species alone
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference only states Lotka-Volterra predicts cyclical oscillations and that lynx/hare show ~10-year cycles from Hudson's Bay fur records; it does not mention additional species or complicating factors.
issues.suggested_fix: Drop or soften the added caveat, or mark it as instructional context rather than a referenced fact.

issues.unsupported_claim: Müllerian mimicry is effectively mutualistic (+/+); Batesian is effectively parasitic (+/−)
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference defines Batesian (harmless mimics harmful) and Müllerian (multiple harmful species share warning patterns) but does not assign the parasitic/mutualistic +/− framing.
issues.suggested_fix: Acceptable pedagogical extension of the defined sign system; optionally flag as interpretation, not a reference claim.

issues.unsupported_claim: OpenStax Biology Unit 8, Khan Academy AP Biology Unit 8, GIIS Unit 8 problem set, Module 14 — Ecosystems and Energy Flow
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference text contains no resource recommendations or next-module titles.
issues.suggested_fix: Course-logistics content outside the reference scope; no factual conflict, leave as-is.

overall_assessment: All scientific specifics — the six interactions and signs, Gause/competitive exclusion, MacArthur's warblers in a spruce tree, fundamental vs realized niche, Lotka-Volterra and lynx/hare ~10-year Hudson's Bay cycles, cryptic/aposematic/Batesian (viceroy/monarch reconsidered)/Müllerian, poison dart frogs, Robert Paine removing Pisaster ochraceus → mussel monoculture, Yellowstone wolves top-down trophic cascade, primary vs secondary succession, richness/evenness/Shannon H, the 10–15% weighting — match the reference. No fabricated dates, numbers, names, or binomials. Only added interpretive framing and standard course-logistics resources go beyond the reference, none of which contradict it.
